implementation_scope: Develop a background task system for AI interpretation runs (D001).
Implement a notification system to alert users upon AI interpretation task completion (D002).
Integrate support for two image sources: a large-scale nationwide batch (Vendor A) and individual user uploads (D004).
Develop a UI for users to confirm suspected points generated by AI interpretation (yes/no) (D005).
Implement functionality allowing users to decide whether to place confirmed suspected points under watch/monitoring (D006).
Design and implement input fields for 'reason' and 'polygon upload' when placing a point under monitoring (D007).
Implement backend validation logic for polygon sizes, filtering out overly small areas (D008).
Develop a duplicate prevention mechanism for large image file uploads (D009).
Implement a manual annotation interface for two-period image comparison (D012).
Develop integration logic for the image processing workflow: client's AP sends images, then calls the on-site workstation to execute the processing model (D014).
Build a map viewer component capable of loading and displaying historically uploaded imagery (D016).
Add filtering capabilities to the map viewer for imagery by date and image-name keyword (D017).
Add filtering capabilities to the map viewer for imagery by type (satellite, aerial photo, drone) (D018).
Implement point rendering functionality within the map viewer (D019).
Add a 'locate' button in the map viewer when a point is opened (D020).
Implement automatic map zooming to a point's location upon 'locate' button activation (D021).
Develop logic for the map viewer to revert to point display when zoomed out past a predefined scale (D022).
Implement map view locking functionality in comparison mode to prevent panning (D023).
Add a 'release drag mode' button in comparison mode to restore map panning (D024).
Implement user-adjustable image transparency functionality in the map viewer (D025).
Implement a data model and UI organization where suspected points are managed within projects, organized yearly (D027, D031).
Develop CRUD functionality for creating new suspected points within existing projects (D028).
Implement mandatory polygon upload for suspected points, without requiring image uploads (D030).
Add a 'New Survey Point Data' interface (D032).
Include 'drawing number' and 'key-monitoring-zone' fields in the 'New Survey Point Data' interface (D033).
Redesign the user interface for the migrated MS file management system, retaining original functionality (D036).
Develop integration for selecting follow-up items from suspected points into a dedicated follow-up module (D039).
Add a schedule-tracking interface to the follow-up module (e.g., displaying days remaining) (D040).
Add a follow-up dispatch interface (D041).
Implement integration between the follow-up module and survey-report management upon follow-up completion (D043).
Develop survey-report management system components to include fields for time, description, action taken, and uploaded images (D044).

constraints.text: Image files are large, necessitating efficient handling and storage (D010).
System requires setup and operation on an on-site workstation (D013).
Base map data must be sourced from GIS Provider B via GeoServer (D015).
Map viewer design must reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System (D026).
'New Survey Point Data' interface design must refer to manual figure 2.13 (D034).
Migration of the legacy MIS system is driven by information-security compliance requirements (D037).
The data flow for AI interpretation must cover: image source → interpretation task execution (workstation) → result output → monitoring database (D047).
Project is currently in a bidding process, with an anticipated kick-off in mid-May if successful (D048, D049).
A potential immediate need for requirements interview and interface confirmation upon bid award (D050).
Sherry is tasked with completing the 'add suspected point' data interface by 2026-05-18 (D051).
System architecture discussion meeting scheduled for 2026-05-15 (D054).
System architecture and data flow diagrams are required for requirements interview preparation (D057, D058).

architectural_decisions.text: Two-period image comparison will no longer pull from an existing image library (D011).
The original MS file management system will be migrated to the new system (D035).

open_technical_questions: What are the specific technical details for the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism (D003)?
Should suspected point layers be split by county/city, and what are the architectural implications (D029)?
What are the specific interface design details for the migrated MIS system, given unchanged functionality (D038)?

acceptance_criteria: Given an AI interpretation run is initiated, Then exactly one background task associated with this run is created and visible in the task list.
Given an AI interpretation task completes its background processing, Then a notification is sent to the user.
Given the option to initiate an AI interpretation, Then the user can select imagery from Vendor A's nationwide batch.
Given the option to initiate an AI interpretation, Then the user can upload individual images.
Given an AI interpretation process has finished, Then a list of suspected points is generated and displayed to the user.
Given a list of suspected points is displayed, When the user interacts with a point, Then the user can explicitly confirm or deny the point (yes/no).
Given a suspected point has been confirmed by the user, Then the user can choose to place this confirmed point under watch/monitoring.

Given a user attempts to place a suspected point under monitoring, When the user does not provide a reason, Then the system prevents the point from being placed under monitoring and prompts for a reason.

Given a user attempts to place a suspected point under monitoring, When the user does not upload an associated polygon, Then the system prevents the point from being placed under monitoring and prompts for a polygon.
Given a user attempts to place a suspected point under monitoring, When the user provides a reason and uploads an associated polygon, Then the point is successfully placed under monitoring.
Given a user uploads a polygon for a monitoring point, When the polygon defines a large area, Then the system accepts the polygon as valid.
Given an image is uploaded for AI interpretation, When the same image is attempted to be uploaded again, Then the system prevents the duplicate upload.
Given the two-period image comparison feature, When attempting to retrieve images, Then the system does not pull from the existing image library.
Given the two-period image comparison feature, Then the user is able to manually annotate images for comparison.
Given an image is initiated for processing via the client's AP, Then the processing model is executed on the workstation.
Given the map viewer is loaded, Then the base map displayed is sourced from GIS Provider B via GeoServer.
Given historically uploaded imagery exists, Then the map viewer can successfully load and display this imagery.
Given imagery is displayed in the map viewer, Then the user can filter the imagery list by date.
Given imagery is displayed in the map viewer, Then the user can filter the imagery list by keywords within the image name.
Given imagery is displayed in the map viewer, Then the user can filter the imagery list by image type (satellite, aerial photo, drone).
Given points are available, Then the map viewer visually represents these points on the map interface.
Given a point is selected or opened on the map viewer, Then a 'locate' button is displayed.
Given a 'locate' button is available for a point, When the user activates the 'locate' button, Then the map automatically adjusts its zoom level and centers on that specific point location.
Given the map displays imagery, When the map is zoomed out beyond a predefined scale, Then the displayed imagery automatically switches to a point-based representation.
Given the map viewer is in comparison mode, Then the user is unable to pan the map view.
Given the map viewer is in comparison mode, Then a 'release drag mode' button is visible.
Given the map viewer is in comparison mode with panning locked, When the user clicks the 'release drag mode' button, Then panning functionality is restored to the map.
Given an image is displayed in the map viewer, Then the user can adjust the transparency level of the image.
Given suspected point management, Then projects are organized and displayed on a yearly basis.
Given an existing project for suspected points, Then a user can create new suspected points and associate them with that project.
Given a user attempts to create a new suspected point, When an uploaded polygon is not provided, Then the system prevents creation and prompts for a polygon.
Given suspected points data for multiple years, Then each year's data forms a distinct layer in the suspected point management interface.
Given the system navigation, Then a new interface titled 'New Survey Point Data' is accessible.
Given the 'New Survey Point Data' interface is accessed, Then it includes a field for 'drawing number'.
Given the 'New Survey Point Data' interface is accessed, Then it includes a field for 'key-monitoring-zone'.
Given the migrated MS file management system, Then its core functionality operates identically to the original system.
Given the migrated MS file management system, Then its user interface has a redesigned appearance.
Given a list of suspected points, Then the user can select specific points for follow-up.
Given selected suspected points for follow-up, Then these points are integrated into the dedicated follow-up module.
Given the follow-up module, Then a schedule-tracking interface is available.
Given the schedule-tracking interface, Then it displays information such as 'days remaining' for follow-up tasks.
Given the follow-up module, Then a follow-up dispatch interface is accessible.
Given a follow-up action is completed, Then the system automatically integrates the completed follow-up data with the survey-report management system.
Given the survey-report management system, Then it includes a field for 'time'.
Given the survey-report management system, Then it includes a field for 'description'.
Given the survey-report management system, Then it includes a field for 'action taken'.
Given the survey-report management system, Then it includes a field for 'uploaded images'.
test_scenarios: Verify that initiating an AI interpretation run creates exactly one background task visible in the task list.
Verify that a user receives a notification upon successful completion of an AI interpretation task.
Verify the ability to initiate AI interpretation using Vendor A's nationwide batch imagery.
Verify the ability to initiate AI interpretation by uploading individual image files.
After AI interpretation, verify that a list of suspected points is generated and displayed.
Verify that a user can confirm (yes) or deny (no) individual suspected points from the generated list.
Verify that a user can place a confirmed suspected point under monitoring by providing a reason and uploading a valid polygon.
Verify that the system accepts large-area polygons for monitoring points.
Verify that the two-period image comparison feature does not pull images from the old image library.
Verify that the user can manually annotate images for two-period comparison.
Verify that the map viewer successfully loads and displays historically uploaded imagery.
Verify that the imagery list in the map viewer can be filtered by date.
Verify that the imagery list in the map viewer can be filtered by keywords present in the image name.
Verify that the imagery list in the map viewer can be filtered by image type (e.g., satellite, aerial photo, drone).
Verify that points are visually represented on the map interface in the map viewer.
Verify that a 'locate' button appears when a point is opened/selected on the map.
Verify that clicking the 'locate' button automatically zooms the map to the selected point's location.
Verify that when the map is zoomed out beyond a predefined scale, imagery automatically switches to a point-based display.
Verify that the map view is locked and cannot be panned when in comparison mode.
Verify that clicking the 'release drag mode' button in comparison mode restores map panning functionality.
Verify that the user can adjust the transparency of images displayed in the map viewer.
Verify that suspected point management projects are organized by year.
Verify that new suspected points can be created and successfully associated with an existing project.
Verify that the 'New Survey Point Data' interface is accessible and contains 'drawing number' and 'key-monitoring-zone' fields.
Verify that the migrated MS file management system retains all core functionalities of the original system.
Verify that items selected from suspected points are successfully integrated into the follow-up module.
Verify that the follow-up module includes a schedule-tracking interface displaying information like 'days remaining'.
Verify that a follow-up dispatch interface is present and accessible.
Verify that upon completion of a follow-up action, the data is correctly integrated with the survey-report management system.
Verify that the survey-report management system includes fields for 'time', 'description', 'action taken', and 'uploaded images'.
edge_cases: Attempt to initiate AI interpretation with corrupted or invalid image files (both batch and individual upload).
Attempt to place a suspected point under monitoring without providing a reason.
Attempt to place a suspected point under monitoring without uploading an associated polygon.
Attempt to upload a polygon for a monitoring point that defines an 'overly small area' and verify it's automatically filtered out (requires clarification on 'overly small').
Attempt to upload an image that is an exact duplicate of a previously uploaded image for AI interpretation.
Attempt to create a new suspected point without uploading a polygon.
Test map viewer behavior when zooming out to just above and just below the predefined scale threshold for reverting to point display.
Test map viewer behavior with an extremely large number of points loaded on the map.
Attempt to pan the map in comparison mode without first activating the 'release drag mode' button.
Apply filters (date, image-name keyword, type) to the map viewer imagery list with no matching results.
Apply filters with empty or malformed input for date or image-name keyword.
Verify behavior when user attempts to upload an image during suspected point creation, despite it not being required (D030).
unresolved_behaviors: Clarify the specific mechanism (e.g., in-app, email, push, toast message) and content/format of the notification sent upon AI interpretation task completion (PM/QA owned).
Clarify whether the layers for suspected points should be divided by county/city (PM/QA owned).
Clarify the specific interface design details for the migrated MS file management system (PM/QA owned).
Clarify who receives follow-up notifications and according to what schedule (PM/QA owned).

decision_history: It was decided that each AI interpretation run will create a single background task, similar to the existing CC platform's operation (D001).
A notification will be sent to the user upon completion of an AI interpretation task (D002).
The system will support two image sources for AI interpretation: a one-time large-scale nationwide batch from Vendor A and individual user-uploaded images (D004).
After AI interpretation, users will receive a list of suspected points and can confirm them (yes/no) (D005).

Users can decide to place confirmed suspected points under watch/monitoring (D006). When placing a point under monitoring, a reason must be provided, and an associated polygon uploaded (D007). The system will validate polygon sizes, accepting large areas and filtering out overly small ones (D008).
A duplicate prevention mechanism is required for image uploads due to their large file size (D009, D010).
The process for two-period image comparison will be adjusted: it will no longer pull images from the existing image library (D011). Instead, users will manually annotate images for comparison (D012).

An on-site workstation will be established and configured to support certain system functions (D013). Images will be sent to the client's Access Point (AP), which will then call the workstation to execute the processing model (D014).
The base map will be provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer (D015).
The map viewer must be capable of loading historically uploaded imagery (D016) and will support filtering by date, image-name keyword (D017), and image type (satellite, aerial photo, drone) (D018).

The map viewer will display points on the map (D019). When a point is opened, a 'locate' button will appear, and activating it will automatically zoom the map to that location (D020, D021). Imagery will revert to point display when zoomed out past a certain scale (D022).

In comparison mode, the map view will be locked (panning prevented), but a 'release drag mode' button will be provided to restore panning (D023, D024). Users can also adjust image transparency for orthophoto analysis (D025). The map viewer's design should reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System (D026).

Suspected point management will organize projects by year (D027), allowing suspected points to be created within projects (D028). Each year will form a distinct layer for suspected points (D031). Creating suspected points requires an uploaded polygon, but no image upload is needed (D030).
A 'New Survey Point Data' interface must be added (D032), including fields for drawing number and key-monitoring-zone, referencing manual figure 2.13 for its design (D033, D034).

The original MS file management system will be migrated to the new system (D035) to meet information-security compliance requirements (D037). The user interface will be redesigned, but core functionality will remain unchanged (D036).

Follow-up items can be selected from suspected points and integrated into a dedicated follow-up module (D039). This module requires a schedule-tracking interface (e.g., showing days remaining) (D040) and a follow-up dispatch interface (D041).
After follow-up completion, the process must connect to survey-report management, which includes fields for time, description, action taken, and uploaded images (D043, D044).

A-Bao requested a data-flow document explaining how data moves after AI interpretation, covering data source, computation location, and the flow from image source to monitoring database via workstation execution and result output (D045, D046, D047).
dependencies: The 'Follow-up dispatch interface' development is dependent on Shao-Chien's first draft (D042).
Project preparation for the requirements interview is dependent on Sherry organizing existing interfaces (including Shao-Chien's parts) (D056, D052).
Project preparation for the requirements interview requires a system architecture diagram (D057). (Owned by SA/Engineering, PM is blocked by its completion).
Project preparation for the requirements interview is dependent on Sherry preparing the data-flow document for A-Bao's review (D058, D053).
A-Bao requires a data-flow document explaining AI interpretation data movement, including source and computation location (D045, D046).
risks: The project is currently in the bidding process (D048). This poses a significant risk to the project timeline as kick-off is contingent on a successful bid, which could lead to delays or project cancellation.

There is a risk of an immediate requirements interview and interface confirmation being needed on the afternoon of bid award (D050). This could lead to rushed and potentially incomplete requirements gathering if not adequately prepared for in advance. (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: High).
open_questions: What are the specific details regarding the notification mechanism for AI interpretation task completion? (D003)
Should the layers for suspected points be divided by county/city? (D029)
What are the specific details for the legacy MIS system migration, particularly regarding interface design? (D038)
Who will receive follow-up notifications and on what schedule? (D059)
milestones: Project expected to kick off in mid-May if bidding successful (D049).
Next work meeting scheduled for 2026-05-15 to discuss system architecture (D054).
Sherry to finish the 'add suspected point' data interface by 2026-05-18 (D051).
Sherry to prepare the data-flow document for A-Bao to review by 2026-05-18 (D053).
Sherry to organize existing interfaces for use in the requirements interview by 2026-05-20 (D052).

system_requirements: The system SHALL generate a single background task for each AI interpretation run, similar to the CC platform's operation (D001).
The system SHALL send a notification to the user upon the completion of an AI interpretation task (D002).
The system SHALL support two distinct image sources for AI interpretation: a one-time large-scale nationwide batch from Vendor A, and individual images uploaded by the user (D004).
After AI interpretation completes, the system SHALL generate a list of suspected points for user review (D005).
The system SHALL allow users to confirm (yes/no) suspected points identified by AI interpretation (D005).
The system SHALL allow users to decide whether to place confirmed suspected points under watch/monitoring (D006).
When placing a suspected point under monitoring, the system SHALL require the user to provide a reason (D007).
When placing a suspected point under monitoring, the system SHALL require the user to upload an associated polygon (D007).
The system SHALL automatically filter out overly small polygon areas provided for monitoring points (D008).
The system SHALL implement a mechanism to prevent duplicate image uploads (D009).
The system SHALL enable users to manually annotate images for two-period comparison (D012).
The system SHALL route uploaded images to the client's Access Point (AP), which then SHALL initiate a call to the on-site workstation to execute the processing model (D014).
The map viewer SHALL be capable of loading and displaying historically uploaded imagery (D016).
The map viewer SHALL support filtering of imagery lists by date (D017).
The map viewer SHALL support filtering of imagery lists by image-name keyword (D017).
The map viewer SHALL support filtering of imagery lists by image type, including satellite, aerial photo, and drone imagery (D018).
The map viewer SHALL display points on the map interface (D019).
When a point is opened on the map viewer, the system SHALL display a 'locate' button (D020).
Upon activation of the 'locate' button, the map SHALL automatically zoom to the specific location of the selected point (D021).
When the map is zoomed out beyond a predefined scale, the displayed imagery SHALL automatically revert to a point-based representation (D022).
In comparison mode, the map view SHALL be locked, preventing user panning (D023).
In comparison mode, the system SHALL provide a 'release drag mode' button to restore panning functionality (D024).
The system SHALL allow users to adjust the transparency of displayed images (D025).
The system SHALL organize projects for suspected point management on a yearly basis (D027).
The system SHALL allow the creation of new suspected points within an existing project (D028).
When creating suspected points, the system SHALL require an uploaded polygon (D030).
The system SHALL NOT require an image upload when creating suspected points (D030).
For suspected point management, each year SHALL constitute a distinct layer (D031).
The system SHALL include a new interface titled 'New Survey Point Data' (D032).
The 'New Survey Point Data' interface SHALL include fields for drawing number (D033).
The 'New Survey Point Data' interface SHALL include fields for key-monitoring-zone (D033).
The user interface for the migrated MS file management system SHALL be redesigned, while its core functionality SHALL remain unchanged (D036).
The system SHALL comply with information-security requirements for the migrated MS file management system (inferred from D037).
The system SHALL allow selection of items from suspected points for integration into the follow-up module (D039).
The follow-up module SHALL include a schedule-tracking interface that displays information such as days remaining (D040).
The system SHALL include a follow-up dispatch interface (D041).
Upon completion of a follow-up action, the system SHALL integrate with the survey-report management system (D043).
The survey-report management system SHALL include fields for time, description, action taken, and uploaded images (D044).
data_flows: AI Interpretation Input: User-uploaded individual images OR Batch imagery from Vendor A -> AI Interpretation module (D004, D047).
Image Processing Workflow: Images -> Client's Access Point (AP) -> On-site Workstation (runs processing model) (D014, D047).
AI Interpretation Output: Interpretation task execution (workstation) -> Result output (list of suspected points) -> Monitoring database (D005, D047).
Follow-up Process: Suspected points selection -> Follow-up module -> Survey-report management system (upon completion) (D039, D043).
interface_contracts: Interface: 'New Survey Point Data' (D032). Expected fields: 'drawing number', 'key-monitoring-zone' (D033). Design reference: Manual Figure 2.13 (D034).
Interface: Follow-up dispatch interface (D041). (A first draft from Shao-Chien exists) (D042).
Interface: Survey-report management system (D043). Expected fields: 'time', 'description', 'action taken', 'uploaded images' (D044).

constraints.text: Image files are large, necessitating efficient handling and duplicate prevention (D010).
An on-site workstation must be established and configured to support certain system functions, specifically image processing (D013, D014, D047).
The base map data for the system must be sourced from GIS Provider B via GeoServer (D015).
The design of the map viewer SHALL reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System (D026).
The design of the 'New Survey Point Data' interface SHALL refer to manual figure 2.13 (D034, D051).
The migration of the legacy MIS system is driven by information-security compliance requirements (D037).

architectural_decisions.text: The two-period image comparison feature will no longer retrieve images from the existing image library (D011).
The existing MS file management system will be migrated to the new system being developed (D035).

open_analysis_questions: What are the specific details of the notification mechanism for AI interpretation task completion (e.g., protocol, content, recipient types)? (D003)
Should the layers for suspected points be divided by county/city? This will impact data storage and retrieval strategy (D029).
What are the detailed data migration strategies and requirements for the legacy MIS system, beyond UI redesign? (D038)
Who are the intended recipients of follow-up notifications, and what is the required schedule/frequency for these notifications? (D059)

stakeholder.business_summary: This project focuses on building an AI-powered system to detect illegal activities, specifically illegal excavations. It will automate image analysis, provide a new map viewer to manage suspected locations, and integrate a secure system for tracking follow-up actions, alongside upgrading an older file management system for improved security.
key_impact: Faster and more efficient identification of potential illegal activities through automated AI analysis.
Improved ability to manage and monitor suspected sites using an enhanced map viewer and dedicated follow-up tools.
Enhanced information security and compliance by upgrading and migrating our legacy file management system.
Streamlined user workflows for reviewing AI findings, confirming suspected points, and initiating necessary follow-up work.
decisions_needing_alignment: The specific way users will be notified when AI analysis is complete.
Whether suspected point layers on the map should be organized by county or city.
The detailed approach and design for migrating the old file management system.
Who should receive follow-up notifications and on what schedule.
major_unresolved_questions: What is the preferred method for notifying users when AI analysis results are ready?
Should we organize suspected points on the map by geographical areas like counties or cities?
What are the specific requirements for redesigning the interface of the migrated file management system?
Who needs to receive notifications for follow-up actions, and what is the desired notification schedule?

issues.description: The 'decisions_needing_alignment' and 'major_unresolved_questions' sections contain identical content, leading to redundancy.
issues.suggestion: Consolidate these two sections or rephrase one to offer a different perspective (e.g., focus one on immediate decisions and the other on broader strategic questions) for better clarity and conciseness.

review.reviewer_notes: All role outputs are well-aligned with the extracted decisions, and no critical hallucinations or misrepresentations were found. There is a minor redundancy in the Stakeholder View that could be improved for conciseness.
